summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API, which enables real-time communication like video calls, include several important improvements and fixes. One significant change is the **enablement of transport-cc for audio codecs**, allowing better control and quality of audio streams during calls by adapting to changing network conditions. Additionally, updates such as ensuring closed updates are fired for transports that have been torn down, even if inactive, help in maintaining accurate connection status reporting. These enhancements not only improve the reliability and efficiency of WebRTC implementations but also streamline the underlying code management for future development, making it easier to maintain and extend this essential feature for modern web applications.
